blocks.content:
This
                                                                                                            document
                                                                                                            sets out how
                                                                                                            a requestor
                                                                                                            should
                                                                                                            construct a
                                                                                                            jwt-auth
                                                                                                            header and
                                                                                                            how a
                                                                                                            receiver
                                                                                                            must
                                                                                                            validate the
                                                                                                            header.

sections.heading:
2.
                                                                                                                Requirements
                                                                                                                for the
                                                                                                                LFI as
                                                                                                                the
                                                                                                                Client
                                                                                                                (sending
                                                                                                                a token)

blocks.content:
These
                                                                                                            examples are
                                                                                                            specific to
                                                                                                            the requests
                                                                                                            from the LFI
                                                                                                            to the API
                                                                                                            Hub CM and
                                                                                                            HH
                                                                                                            endpoints.

sections.heading:
2.1
                                                                                                                Publication
                                                                                                                of
                                                                                                                signing
                                                                                                                keys on
                                                                                                                a JWKS

blocks.content:
The
                                                                                                                    LFI
                                                                                                                    must
                                                                                                                    create
                                                                                                                    an
                                                                                                                    Application
                                                                                                                    in
                                                                                                                    their
                                                                                                                    OFTF
                                                                                                                    Organisation
                                                                                                                    labelled
                                                                                                                    C3-hh-cm-client.

The
                                                                                                                    LFI
                                                                                                                    must
                                                                                                                    create
                                                                                                                    both
                                                                                                                    the
                                                                                                                    C3
                                                                                                                    transport
                                                                                                                    client
                                                                                                                    and
                                                                                                                    Sig4
                                                                                                                    transport
                                                                                                                    signing
                                                                                                                    certificates
                                                                                                                    in
                                                                                                                    this
                                                                                                                    Application
                                                                                                                    using
                                                                                                                    code
                                                                                                                    snippets
                                                                                                                    provided
                                                                                                                    in
                                                                                                                    the
                                                                                                                    OFTF.

The
                                                                                                                    OFTF
                                                                                                                    will
                                                                                                                    create
                                                                                                                    a
                                                                                                                    unique
                                                                                                                    KID
                                                                                                                    and
                                                                                                                    host
                                                                                                                    the
                                                                                                                    JWKS.

When
                                                                                                                    publishing
                                                                                                                    a
                                                                                                                    new
                                                                                                                    key,
                                                                                                                    the
                                                                                                                    LFI
                                                                                                                    must
                                                                                                                    wait
                                                                                                                    for
                                                                                                                    10
                                                                                                                    minutes
                                                                                                                    before
                                                                                                                    issuing
                                                                                                                    a
                                                                                                                    message
                                                                                                                    with
                                                                                                                    the
                                                                                                                    key
                                                                                                                    (to
                                                                                                                    allow
                                                                                                                    the
                                                                                                                    receiver’s
                                                                                                                    caches
                                                                                                                    to
                                                                                                                    refresh).
The LFI must ensure that the machine on which the signature is generated uses NTP to synchronise its clock.
The LFI must construct the header and payload for the JWT as specified in JWT Authorization | 3. JWT Auth Claims Reference  .

blocks.text: JWT Authorization | 3. JWT Auth Claims Reference

blocks.content: The JWT must be signed  using the PS256 algorithm using a private signing key in the C3-hh-cm-client application.
The JWT must be included as a bearer token in the authorization http header.
The https request must be made over mutual tls. The C3 client certificate must be used to initiate the mutual tls session, and must have a DN and OU that matches the values placed in the signature.

sections.heading: 3. Requirements for the LFI as the Server (receiving a token)

blocks.content: These examples are specific to the requests from the API Hub to the LFI Ozone Connect API.
The receiver must ensure that the machine on which the signature is verified uses NTP to synchronise its clock.
The receiver must ensure that the request was received over a mutual tls connection.
The receiver must extract the jwt-auth token from the authorization http header.
The JWT must verify the signature on the JWT using the kid specified in the JWS
The JWKS URI is derived from the requestor client certificate subject. The OU and CN values are substituted as follows:
Example requestor client certificate subject CN=ABC, OU=XYZ, O=Organisation Name, C=AE
Template for Sandbox JWKS https://keystore.sandbox.directory.openfinance.ae/${OU}/${CN}/application.jwks
Example JWKS URI: https://keystore.sandbox.directory.openfinance.ae/XYZ/ABC/application.jwks
The receiver may cache the JWKS for up to ten minutes.
The receiver must verify each of the claims in the JWT has the expected value specified in JWT Authorization | 3. JWT Auth Claims Reference

blocks.text: JWT Authorization | 3. JWT Auth Claims Reference

content: The key ID of key-pair used to sign the message as published on the JWKS.
The specification does not support the use of other means of identifying the key as they are not considered to be secure enough (e.g. x5c and x5u are not supported).

blocks.content: Claim Name
Expected Value
Mandatory
Notes
alg
PS256
Yes
typ
JOSE
Yes
cty
json
Yes
kid
The key ID of key-pair used to sign the message as published on the JWKS.
Yes
The specification does not support the use of other means of identifying the key as they are not considered to be secure enough (e.g. x5c and x5u are not supported).

content: Example value for JWT issued by Ozone
The organization O in the TLS certificate Subject that was used in the transport layer.
For a certificate with Subject ofCN=ABC, OU=XYZ, O=RAIDIAM SERVICES LIMITED, C=UK,this would be set to RAIDIAM SERVICES LIMITED
The organization unit OU in the TLS certificate Subject that was used in the transport layer.
For a certificate with Subject of CN=ABC, OU=XYZ, O=RAIDIAM SERVICES LIMITED, C=UK, this would be set to XYZ
Identifier for the party receiving the JWT.
This must be set to the PROVIDER_ID specified by Ozone during configuration.
Time when the JWT will expire in UTC seconds since epoch.
We recommend an expiry time of 10-30s.When validating the JWT, allow for a 10s clock skew.The JWT is considered invalid if the current time is greater than this value.
Time when the JWT was issued in UTC seconds since epoch.
When validating the JWT, allow for a 10s clock skew.The JWT is considered invalid if the current time is less than this value.
Time before which the JWT is invalid.
When validating the JWT, allow for a 10s clock skew.The JWT is considered invalid if the current time is less than this value (when specified).
A unique identifier for the JWT.
We recommend that the requestor populates this value with a uuidv4. This increases the entropy for the JWT.

blocks.content: Claim Name
Expected Value
Mandatory
Example value for JWT issued by Ozone
Notes
iss
The organization O in the TLS certificate Subject that was used in the transport layer.
Yes
Ozone Financial Technology Limited
For a certificate with Subject ofCN=ABC, OU=XYZ, O=RAIDIAM SERVICES LIMITED, C=UK,this would be set to RAIDIAM SERVICES LIMITED
sub
The organization unit OU in the TLS certificate Subject that was used in the transport layer.
Yes
For a certificate with Subject of CN=ABC, OU=XYZ, O=RAIDIAM SERVICES LIMITED, C=UK, this would be set to XYZ
aud
Identifier for the party receiving the JWT.
Yes
This must be set to the PROVIDER_ID specified by Ozone during configuration.
exp
Time when the JWT will expire in UTC seconds since epoch.
Yes
We recommend an expiry time of 10-30s.
When validating the JWT, allow for a 10s clock skew.
The JWT is considered invalid if the current time is greater than this value.
iat
Time when the JWT was issued in UTC seconds since epoch.
Yes
When validating the JWT, allow for a 10s clock skew.
The JWT is considered invalid if the current time is less than this value.
nbf
Time before which the JWT is invalid.
No
When validating the JWT, allow for a 10s clock skew.
The JWT is considered invalid if the current time is less than this value (when specified).
jti
A unique identifier for the JWT.
Yes
We recommend that the requestor populates this value with a uuidv4. This increases the entropy for the JWT.
